content:
First, note that 391 = 17⋅23.  For a fixed a<391, one has

   lcm(a, 391)  =  a·391 / gcd(a, 391),  
 lcm(a, b)   =  a·b   / gcd(a, b).

So we want some a<391 and b>0 with
 a·b/gcd(a,b)  >  a·391/gcd(a,391)
⇔  b⋅gcd(a,391)  >  391⋅gcd(a,b).  

We look for the smallest b for which this can happen.  Trying b=2,3,… shows none work until

b=18:

Take a=23 (<391).  Then gcd(a,391)=23, gcd(a,18)=1, and
 lcm(23,18)=23·18=414,
 lcm(23,391)=391·23/23=391,
so indeed 414>391.  No smaller b admits a solution.  

Hence the minimum possible b is 

\boxed{18}.

   lcm(a, 391)  =  a·391 / gcd(a, 391),

 a·b/gcd(a,b)  >  a·391/gcd(a,391)
⇔  b⋅gcd(a,391)  >  391⋅gcd(a,b).  

We look for the smallest b for which this can happen.  Trying b=2,3,… shows none work until

b=18:

Take a=23 (<391).  Then gcd(a,391)=23, gcd(a,18)=1, and
 lcm(23,18)=23·18=414,
 lcm(23,391)=391·23/23=391,
so indeed 414>391.  No smaller b admits a solution.  

Hence the minimum possible b is 

\boxed{18}.
